checks.finding: T-001 is appropriately medium for observable full-profile generation behavior; T-002 is appropriately high for fail-closed filesystem-boundary validation; T-003 is appropriately high for review-input integrity and approval-boundary changes; T-004 is appropriately low for synchronized metadata and changelog updates.

In tasks.md, high-risk T-002 and T-003 each require only 'A distinct independent reviewer verdict' in Done When and do not name the required second reviewer identity or role. The HIGH-CRITICAL-EVIDENCE contract explicitly requires a named second reviewer, not the implementing agent. Without that inspectable assignment, the mandatory independent-review evidence cannot be verified by the downstream high-risk gate; this is a Critical hard-policy violation.

T-001 is oversized: its Scope combines at least five distinct implementation areas—four layer templates, cross-layer design/traceability templates, the bilingual question bank, the visualization guide, and interviewer/run instructions—plus their acceptance fixtures. This exceeds the stated three-area sizing indicator and makes one-session implementation and isolated verification unrealistic, creating a blocking Major decomposition risk.

Functional tasks have explicit error or edge coverage: T-001 maps to existing-file preservation, LITE exclusion, unaffected bugfix/refactor layers, and no-mockup cases; T-002 maps to missing-file, invalid-slug, traversal, parity, and legacy-mode cases; T-003 maps to missing, substituted, tampered, malformed traceability, Draft rejection, and authorized-approval cases.
SKIP: no standalone test task declaring unit, integration, end-to-end, or acceptance scope exists; testing is embedded in the four implementation tasks.
High-risk T-002 and T-003 both contain explicit Rollback fields and require rollback exercise against isolated fixtures in Done When.

No uncoordinated same-purpose file overlap is declared: T-001 owns layer/interviewer artifacts, T-002 owns structure-check scripts, T-003 owns review-gate integrity artifacts, and T-004 owns release metadata after its blockers complete.

T-003 legitimately depends on T-001 because its review manifests and traceability checks consume the layer artifacts T-001 creates. T-004 legitimately depends on T-001, T-002, and T-003 because release publication follows verification of all additive capability areas. T-001 and T-002 are logically independent and correctly have no blockers.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope.
